About me

Story time...

Originally from Estonia, growing up in a tiny village, where the long fields and the forest accompanied most of my childhood. Little did I know where the journey would lead me…

After finishing highschool and wondering, “oh my, what am I gonna do with my life from now on”, I got accepted in Tallinn University of Technology or TalTech as they like to tall themself now, we called it Tipikas. This also meant that I had to move out from my tiny little ecosystem in my version of The Shire and unlike Frodo began my journey without Samwell towards the Mount Doom, which we called Tallinn - the capital of Estonia.

As depicted above, I spent way too much time studying and before finishing my bachelor degree had to due the mandatory military service for 11 months. Now a lot of people complain about this service and say it is complete waste of time. From personal experience I can say that you take life exactly how it presents itself to you and if you do not grab the opportunities which are presented it will never be satisfying. And so was my time in the military service, the first 3 months was super hard. It was most hardest thing to get adjusted to as we were 16 guys in the same room and if one of us got sick all of us were sick. It did not help that the building was from sovetic era. The first 3 months of military service is called SBK and no it’s not Salsa Bachata Kizomba but rather sõdur baaskursus, where the individual fighter tactics is drilled. Which is then followed by another 3 months of group level training, now depending on your background you get specialization for another 3 months and luckily I got chosen also for the platoon level training. The final month of the service is a military exercise called the “Kevadtorm”, which simulates a war game.

In my opinion, the whole experience gave very good self-discipline and good idea how these kind of semi authorian organizations work. I met very good friends, some of which I talk till today and I would never say that this was a waste of time.

[…]

My journey with machine learning began during my studies at the Friedrich-Alexander University in Germany around the beginning of 2015. Back then it was still the early days and I was wondering which direction I should steer with my studies and then I stumbled upon the GoogleNet publication, which sparked me the interest for everything related to machine learning, deep learning, neural networks and so on.

From that point on I steered my electives at the university and my student work projects from ML prospective and got a student job at the International Audio Laboratories Erlangen, a department between the university and the Fraunhofer Institute.

One of the first projects I worked on was an instrument extraction with deep neural networks using stacked auto encoders. I had my first experience using programming languages such as Theano and Lasagne. I also wrote my thesis (MSc) titled “Soft Decision Trees for Singing Voice Detection”, where I was working on was a novel audio domain research question, namely the singing voice classification from an audio frame using decision trees and novel approach of soft-decision trees (a mix of decision trees and neural networks).

After finishing my degree at the end of 2016, I started working at the Fraunhofer Institute on position estimation problems. My work has also received international recognition, first the publication at the Indoor Positioning and Indoor Navigation where I got the best paper award. Followed by extended research publication at the MDPI Sensors.
